# US Stock Watcher — Next-Session Forecast: Tuesday, July 14

**Session outlook: bullish lean — Moderate / selective risk-on (composite +27, confidence 43%)**

The next U.S. regular session is Tuesday, July 14, opening at 9:30 AM ET (22:30 KST). This brief is a forecast for that upcoming session, prepared while markets are closed, and all figures below reflect data as of 2026-07-13T22:10:40 (data quality: fresh).

## Executive Summary

Heading into Tuesday's open, our overall market-state score stands at +27 out of 100, placing the tape in a "Moderate / selective risk-on" posture with 43% confidence, based on four measured components. Several inputs remain unmeasured for this cycle — Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning were reweighted out of the current read — so the signal should be treated as directional rather than comprehensive.

## Market Diagnosis

**Indices.** Coming into the new session, the major benchmarks closed as follows: the S&P 500 at 7,515.34 (-0.79% on the day), the Nasdaq-100 at 29,264.10 (-1.88%), the Dow at 52,498.64 (-0.26%), and the Russell 2000 at 2,953.17 (-0.83%). The broad-based pullback across cap tiers, led by the Nasdaq-100, sets the technical backdrop entering Tuesday's trade.

**Market drivers.** Three of four tracked drivers are supportive heading into the session — volatility, index trend, and the yield curve are all constructive — while the US dollar remains a headwind that could cap upside follow-through.

**Breadth & concentration.** The composite market-state score of +27/100 corroborates the "Moderate / selective risk-on" characterization (43% confidence, four components measured), though the previously noted gaps in Liquidity, Credit, Earnings, Macro surprises, Valuation, and Positioning data limit the depth of this reading.

**Sector & rotation.** Positioning into Tuesday favors selectivity: Financials, Industrials, and Health Care lead, while Real Estate, Consumer Staples, and Energy lag — a rotation pattern consistent with a cautiously pro-cyclical tilt rather than a broad risk-on stampede.

**Rates, dollar & commodities.** The 10-year Treasury yield sits at 4.56%, while the dollar index (DXY) is at 101.29, up 0.32% on the day. Commodities flagged a notable move overnight: WTI crude surged 9.10% to $77.91, a jump likely tied to escalating Middle East tensions noted in today's news flow, while gold slipped 2.25% to 4,011.60.

## Outlook for the Tuesday, July 14 Session

**Short term.** For Tuesday's open specifically, the bias is constructive, consistent with the "Moderate / selective risk-on" market state and its 43% confidence level. This lean would reverse if the overall composite score were to cross back below zero.

**Medium term.** Looking beyond the immediate session, the medium-term stance remains similarly constructive under the same "Moderate / selective risic-on" framework (43% confidence), with the same caveat: a break below zero on the composite score would flip the view.

**Medium-to-long term.** Extending the horizon further, the medium-to-long-term read stays aligned with the constructive bias tied to the current "Moderate / selective risk-on" state (43% confidence), again subject to reversal should the overall score cross zero.

## Top News Shaping the Setup

Several headlines are likely to weigh on sentiment into Tuesday's session. AppLovin has been flagged as one of the worst-performing S&P 500 stocks today (MSN). In Asia, AI-peak fears have slammed South Korean equities as the semiconductor rally falters (Chosunbiz). On the policy front, traders are now pricing a 50% chance that a Warsh-led Fed hikes rates this month (Canadian Mortgage Trends). Geopolitical risk is also in focus, with the TSX and U.S. markets falling as Middle East fighting sends oil prices soaring (Yahoo! Finance Canada) — consistent with the sharp WTI move noted above. On the earnings front, Oppenheimer observes that the S&P 500 heads into second-quarter earnings season with elevated growth targets (marketscreener.com). Finally, a lighter note: a long-overlooked "boring" dividend ETF has quietly crushed the S&P 500 this year (AOL.com).
